segments.text: [Speaker Notes] Chapter 12, Opener, The bubbles and alcohol in this glass of beer are produced by  fermentation, a metabolic process in which yeast cells convert the sugar in the grains to CO2 and ethanol. Fermentation is a way of life for anaerobic microorganisms, but it is also central to the metabolism of  air-breathing animals, in the form of glycolysis.
